alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two dictionary learning algorithms are proposed that adaptively learn continuous basis functions from correlated signals on arbitrary point clouds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OMG-Net significantly outperforms the previous state-of-the-art MIDOG++ benchmark model, with a +16% increase in F1-score for breast cancer detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: OMG-Net outperforms previous state-of-the-art models in hold-out test sets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed AI-aided approach provides superior accuracy in detecting mitotic figures across various types of tumours obtained with different scanners. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the preprint's performance-oriented conclusion as a contribution focused on generalisability and high performance; the substance aligns but is expressed more as a methodological contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings outline a rich research agenda for the networking community. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Prior work in this area suffers from high absolute error percentages when forecasting performance on unseen models and new GPUs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The choice of NN architecture significantly influences the objective landscape and the optimizer’s path to an optimum.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result about architecture affecting landscape and optimizer trajectory.

alignments.pub_claim_text: NNs introduce non-convexities even in otherwise convex landscapes, potentially delaying convergence in convex problems but enhancing exploration for non-convex problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spatial feature regularization significantly improves reconstruction accuracy in DL-based Array-SAR. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of spatial feature regularization retrieves more complete building structures in urban mapping. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning methods in Array-SAR have previously neglected spatial features, leading to artifacts in reconstruction. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert or present an explicit claim that prior deep learning Array-SAR methods neglected spatial features and caused artifacts, so this limitation statement from the preprint is not carried forward.

alignments.rationale: The exploration of fusion is preserved but the publication presents it with lower certainty (low vs medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Xiaomai is designed to help Chinese college students in learning advanced mathematics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The current design of free-response questions and the metacognitive feature of error reflection do not enhance the efficacy of the math ITS.
alignments.rationale: The preprint’s positive framing of Xiaomai’s design is revised in the publication to state that the current design does not enhance efficacy, effectively weakening the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates the incorporation of free-response questions with automatic feedback in Xiaomai.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Students in the multiple-choice conditions demonstrated better practice performance and learning outcomes compared to their counterparts in the free-response conditions.
alignments.rationale: The preprint’s investigation claim is retained but the publication supplies the concrete result (MC > free-response), clarifying the outcome of that investigation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study also examines the metacognitive element of reflecting on self-made errors within Xiaomai.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of error reflection did not yield a significant impact on students’ practice performance or learning outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The preprint’s stated examination of error reflection is present in the publication, which reports the null result for that feature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VarAD achieves superior high-resolution image anomaly detection performance compared to conventional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visual autoregressive modeling-based anomaly detection (VarAD) achieves superior high-resolution image anomaly detection (HRIAD) performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VarAD maintains a lightweight design while addressing the computational burden of high-resolution image anomaly detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VarAD maintains a lightweight design while effectively addressing the computational burden of high-resolution image anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: The design and computational claims are preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method effectively utilizes information from all preceding tokens to enhance the prediction of target tokens.
alignments.rationale: The publication’s listed claims focus on performance and experimental validation and do not state this specific technical mechanism, so the preprint claim is not reflected in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Phoneme durations leak some speaker information and can reveal speaker identity from both original and anonymized speech.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Phoneme durations leak some speaker information and can reveal speaker identity from both original and anonymized speech.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work emphasizes the importance of taking into account the speaker's speech rate and phonetic duration characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work emphasizes the importance of taking into account the speaker’s speech rate and phonetic duration characteristics.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need to modify phonetic duration characteristics to develop anonymization systems with strong privacy protection capacity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need to modify phonetic duration characteristics to develop anonymization systems with strong privacy protection capacity.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for anonymization system changes is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dynamic Channel Pruning is introduced to the audio domain for the first time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dynamic Channel Pruning is introduced to the audio domain for the first time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The wording and contribution are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach saves 29.6% of MACs while only causing a 0.75% drop in PESQ. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach saves up to 32.4% of MACs while only causing a 0.32% drop in PESQ. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical results differ: the publication reports a larger max MACs reduction and a smaller PESQ drop, so the result was revised (stronger/broader performance).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DynCP offers a promising path toward deploying larger and more powerful speech enhancement solutions on resource-constrained devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DynCP offers a promising path toward deploying larger and more powerful speech enhancement solutions on resource-constrained devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried forward verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.rationale: This background/epidemiological statement appears in the preprint but is not present among the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LGBM Classifier and Random Forest emerged as the best models for predicting food processing with F1-scores of 0.9411 and 0.9345, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A user-friendly web server for predicting processing level based on the nutrient panel of a food product has been developed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A user-friendly web server was developed for predicting the processing level based on the nutrient panel of a food product. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MAIDS Model utilizes the XGBoost machine learning classification algorithm to secure data allocation and communication in cloud systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAIDS proactively identifies malicious agents by examining their eligibility for data access, rather than only after data leaks occur. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MAIDS model proactively identifies malicious agents by examining their eligibility for respective data access. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The MAIDS model provides a comprehensive solution to safeguard crucial data from both intentional and non-intentional breaches. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model enhances the system’s security in terms of authorized data access accuracy up to 55.49%, precision up to 43.15%, recall up to 55.49%, and F1-score up to 39.96%, compared to state-of-the-art work. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vieira is a declarative framework that unifies mechanisms to augment foundation models for programming applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Hint loss from the hidden layer of RNN and context vector loss from the attention module enhance the model's prediction performance for identifying at-risk students. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNN-Attention-KD framework facilitates early prediction of at-risk students throughout a course. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel constrained search framework introduced in this paper significantly reduces the constrained search time compared to the state of the art. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CwA-T outperforms baseline models such as EEGNet, Deep4Conv, and FusionCNN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CwE-T achieved 85.0% accuracy, 76.2% sensitivity, and 91.2% specificity for the TUH Abnormal EEG Corpus, outperforming baseline models such as EEGNet, Deep4Conv, and FusionCNN.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional RPA solutions often face performance limitations when processing large volumes of documents, leading to inefficiencies.
alignments.rationale: The limitation described in the preprint is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmark comparisons with leading platforms like UiPath and Automation Anywhere demonstrate the effectiveness of ERPA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Benchmark comparisons with leading platforms like UiPath and Automation Anywhere demonstrate the effectiveness of ERPA.
alignments.rationale: The content of the benchmark claim is unchanged, but the publication marks this claim with higher reported certainty (medium→high), indicating amplified confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning infrared small target detection method combines image super-resolution technology with multi-scale observation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning infrared small target detection method combines image super-resolution technology with multi-scale observation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological combination described in the preprint with no substantive alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new deep-learning network named YOLO-MST includes a self-designed MSFA module and a multi-scale dynamic detection head.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new deep-learning network named YOLO-MST demonstrates improved performance by dynamically fusing features from different scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes dynamic multi-scale feature fusion (a clarification of the functional effect) rather than naming specific modules; the core contribution is preserved but phrased differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mAP@0.5 detection rates of the YOLO-MST method on the SIRST and IRIS datasets reached 96.4% and 99.5% respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mAP@0.5 detection rates of the YOLO-MST method on the datasets IRIS, SIRST, and SIRST+ are 99.5%, 96.4%, and 91.4% respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the reported IRIS and SIRST mAP values from the preprint while adding results on an additional dataset (SIRST+), thereby broadening the empirical scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The YOLO-MST method more effectively solves the problems of missed detection, false alarms, and low precision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The YOLO-MST method effectively solves the problems of missed detection, false alarms, and low precision in infrared small target detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's conclusion about solving detection issues with equivalent strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Manufacturing firms often encounter failures in their B2B platform development initiatives, leading them to abandon initial efforts and pivot to alternative platform strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Manufacturing firms often encounter failures in their B2B platform development initiatives, leading them to abandon initial efforts and pivot to alternative platform strategies.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the finding about failures and pivots in B2B platform initiatives with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study articulates a three-phase pivoting framework for B2B platform business models, including platform development and platform strategy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study articulates a three-phase pivoting framework for B2B platform business models, including platform development and platform strategy.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the three-phase pivoting framework claim from the preprint without alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The manufacturer's shift towards a strategic multi-platform integration into an all-encompassing platform supported by artificial intelligence indicates a maturation of the platform business model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The manufacturer's shift towards a strategic multi-platform integration supported by artificial intelligence signifies a maturation of the platform business model.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same interpretive conclusion about maturation due to AI-supported multi-platform integration with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The firm's platform business model evolution included a deliberate platform strategy open to external stakeholders and enveloping its data-driven offerings within a broader platform ecosystem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final step of the firm's platform business model evolution involved a deliberate platform strategy open to external stakeholders, encompassing its data-driven offerings within a broader platform ecosystem.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same descriptive claim about the final step of platform evolution, preserving meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical structure enhances classification precision by leveraging the strengths of both Transformer Decoder-based and ELMo pre-trained embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical structure enhances classification precision by leveraging the strengths of both Transformer Decoder-based and ELMo pre-trained embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological claim verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical evaluations demonstrate the superiority of the Transformer Decoder embedding in classification tasks compared to existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical evaluations demonstrate the superiority of the Transformer Decoder embedding in classification tasks compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the empirical superiority claim for the Transformer Decoder embedding without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of an hour-of-the-day embedding significantly improves recognition of time-sensitive activities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of an hour-of-the-day embedding into the architecture improves recognition of time-sensitive activities.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly softens wording but preserves the substantive claim that hour-of-the-day embedding improves time-sensitive activity recognition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GPT-inspired hierarchical approach outperforms the state-of-the-art ELMo benchmark in activity recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPT-inspired hierarchical approach outperforms the state-of-the-art ELMo benchmark.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same comparative performance claim against the ELMo benchmark with equivalent strength.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative hierarchical design significantly enhances the efficacy of both pre-trained embeddings in capturing inter-activity nuances.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about enhanced efficacy in capturing inter-activity nuances does not have a counterpart in the preprint and thus appears as an added interpretive/result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMMF reduces the memory requirement of adaptive learning rate optimizers, such as Adam, by up to 96%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMMF reduces the memory requirement of adaptive learning rate optimizers, such as Adam, by up to 96%.
alignments.rationale: The publication text is essentially identical to the preprint claim, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMMF enables flexible and efficient factorization of an arbitrary rank of the first and second momentum tensors during optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMMF enables flexible and efficient factorization of an arbitrary rank of the first and second momentum tensors during optimization.
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMMF converges similarly to non-memory-efficient adaptive learning rate optimizers, providing a theoretical basis for its competitive optimization capability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMMF converges similarly to non-memory-efficient adaptive learning rate optimizers, such as AdamNC.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the convergence claim but drops the explicit "theoretical basis" phrasing and adds a concrete example (AdamNC), clarifying rather than changing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMMF takes up to 96% less memory compared to state-of-the-art memory efficient optimizers while achieving comparable model performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMMF takes up to 96% less memory compared to state-of-the-art memory-efficient optimizers while achieving comparable model performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance and memory-reduction claim with only minor wording/typo normalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed residual channel-based data augmentation strategy significantly enhances feature extraction ability and generalization in Radio Frequency Fingerprint Identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed residual channel-based data augmentation strategy significantly enhances feature extraction ability and generalization in Radio Frequency Fingerprint Identification.
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method requires fewer samples and less time for fine-tuning the pre-trained model in a novel environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method requires fewer samples and less time for fine-tuning the pre-trained model in a novel environment.
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in wording or meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of least square and minimum mean square error channel estimations followed by equalization generates signals with different residual channel effects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of least square and minimum mean square error channel estimations followed by equalization generates signals with different residual channel effects.
alignments.rationale: Claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiQP is a novel Transformer-Diffusion model designed for restoring 8K video quality degraded by codec compression.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiQP is a novel Transformer-Diffusion model designed for restoring 8K video quality degraded by codec compression.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiQP is the first model to restore artifacts introduced by various codecs using Denoising Diffusion without considering additional noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiQP is the first model to restore artifacts introduced by various codecs using Denoising Diffusion without considering additional noise.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture of DiQP effectively learns to reverse the degradation caused by compression artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture of DiQP effectively learns to reverse the degradation caused by the complex, non-Gaussian nature of compression artifacts.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the complex, non-Gaussian character of artifacts, adding precision to the original architectural claim without changing its core meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that DiQP outperforms state-of-the-art methods for high-resolution videos such as 4K and 8K.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that DiQP outperforms state-of-the-art methods for high-resolution videos, particularly 4K and 8K.
alignments.rationale: Experimental performance claim is preserved; the publication emphasizes the same target resolutions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Domain Adaptive LIdar (DALI) object detection framework effectively addresses noise at both distribution and instance levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The domain adaptive LiDAR (DALI) object detection framework effectively addresses noise at both distribution and instance levels.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is unchanged aside from minor orthographic normalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A post-training size normalization (PTSN) strategy is developed to mitigate bias in pseudo label size distribution.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A posttraining size normalization (PTSN) strategy is developed to mitigate bias in pseudolabel size distribution.
alignments.rationale: Same methodological contribution, minor wording differences only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DALI framework achieves state-of-the-art results and outperforms leading approaches on most domain adaptation tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DALI framework achieves state-of-the-art results and outperforms leading approaches on most of the domain adaptation tasks.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim retained with effectively identical meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two pseudopoint clouds generation (PPCG) strategies are developed to ensure consistency between pseudolabels and pseudopoints during training.
alignments.rationale: This PPCG contribution appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims, indicating an added claim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NeRF-NQA is the first no-reference quality assessment method for densely-observed scenes synthesized from Neural View Synthesis and NeRF variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NeRF-NQA is the first no-reference quality assessment method for densely-observed scenes synthesized from Neural View Synthesis and NeRF variants.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NeRF-NQA employs a joint quality assessment strategy that integrates both viewwise and pointwise approaches to evaluate the quality of NVS-generated scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NeRF-NQA employs a joint quality assessment strategy that integrates both viewwise and pointwise approaches to evaluate the quality of NVS-generated scenes.
alignments.rationale: Methodological description is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that NeRF-NQA significantly outperforms existing assessment methods in assessing NVS-synthesized scenes without references.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate that NeRF-NQA significantly outperforms existing assessment methods in assessing NVS-synthesized scenes without references.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing quality assessment methods like PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS are inadequate for capturing the perceptual quality of NVS-synthesized scenes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing quality assessment methods like PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS are inadequate for capturing the perceptual quality of NVS-synthesized scenes.
alignments.rationale: Limitation claim remains the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study documents a significant association between LLM scores and subsequent daily stock returns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPT-3-based OPT model significantly outperforms other models in predicting stock market returns with an accuracy of 74.4%.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the association is driven by the GPT-3-based OPT model and gives an accuracy figure, narrowing and clarifying the original general LLM-to-returns association.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: OPT shows the highest accuracy in sentiment prediction with an accuracy of 74.4%, outperforming BERT and FINBERT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPT-3-based OPT model significantly outperforms other models in predicting stock market returns with an accuracy of 74.4%.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim for OPT (74.4% accuracy) and its superior performance, so this is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Regression analyses highlight a robust positive impact of OPT model scores on next-day stock returns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPT-3-based OPT model significantly outperforms other models in predicting stock market returns with an accuracy of 74.4%.
alignments.rationale: The preprint claims a regression-based positive impact on next-day returns, while the publication emphasizes classification/prediction accuracy for OPT; this alters the nature of the result (regression effect -> predictive accuracy).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Loughran-McDonald dictionary model demonstrates considerably lower effectiveness with only 50.1% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the Loughran-McDonald dictionary or the 50.1% accuracy, so this claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The long-short OPT strategy excels with a Sharpe ratio of 3.05, indicating superior portfolio performance compared to other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A long-short strategy based on the OPT model yields an exceptional Sharpe ratio of 3.05 after accounting for 10 basis points in transaction costs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same Sharpe ratio claim but adds the detail of transaction costs, clarifying rather than changing the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for strictly deterministic initialization of a neural network with one hidden layer for solving physical problems described by an ODE is proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A method for strictly deterministic initialization of a neural network with one hidden layer for solving physical problems described by an ODE is proposed.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same proposed deterministic initialization method without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods for initializing and training neural networks with one or two hidden layers achieve competitive accuracy and, in some cases, state-of-the-art results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods for initializing and training neural networks with one or two hidden layers achieve competitive accuracy and, in some cases, state-of-the-art results.
alignments.rationale: The result about competitive / sometimes SOTA accuracy is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A gradient-free neuron-by-neuron fitting method has been developed for adjusting the parameters of a single-hidden-layer neural network, which does not require the use of an optimizer or solver for its implementation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A gradient-free neuron-by-neuron fitting method has been developed for adjusting the parameters of a single-hidden-layer neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the gradient-free, neuron-by-neuron fitting contribution; wording is essentially the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A metric for measuring the generalization of such neural networks has been introduced.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A metric for measuring the generalization of the neural network has been introduced.
alignments.rationale: The introduced generalization metric is present in the publication with the same meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD) method is a sustainable alternative to Continuous Flooding (CF) for rice-growing water management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD) method is a sustainable alternative to Continuous Flooding (CF) for rice-growing water management.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same sustainability conclusion about AWD as an alternative to CF.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach automates water height measurement using computer vision and a convolutional neural network (CNN).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach using an attention-based ConvNeXt architecture automates water height measurement and overcomes limitations of traditional methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the architecture (attention-based ConvNeXt) and frames automation as overcoming prior limitations, clarifying the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attention-based architecture achieved an $R^2$ score of 0.9885 and a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.2766.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance with an R 2 score of 0.989, a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.523 cm, and a Mean Squared Error (MSE) of 0.277 cm².
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same performance with slight rounding and adds RMSE in units, clarifying and formalizing the metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper is the first to present the use of inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) to learn desired rewards for restless multi-armed bandits (RMABs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper is the first to present the use of inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) to learn desired rewards for restless multi-armed bandits (RMABs).
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the novelty claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate improved outcomes in a maternal and child health telehealth program using our proposed algorithm WHIRL.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate improved outcomes in a maternal and child health telehealth program using the proposed algorithm WHIRL.
alignments.rationale: The reported improved outcomes using WHIRL are preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm WHIRL uses gradient updates to optimize the objective, allowing for efficient and accurate learning of RMAB rewards.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm WHIRL uses gradient updates to optimize the objective, allowing for efficient and accurate learning of RMAB rewards.
alignments.rationale: The methodological claim about gradient updates in WHIRL is carried over identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We compare with existing baselines and outperform those in terms of run-time and accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We compare with existing baselines and outperform those in terms of run-time and accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The comparative performance claim against baselines is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We evaluate and show the usefulness of WHIRL on thousands of beneficiaries from a real-world maternal and child health setting in India.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We evaluate and show the usefulness of WHIRL on thousands of beneficiaries from a real-world maternal and child health setting in India.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation on thousands of real-world beneficiaries is reported the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) demonstrate impressive zero-shot classification capabilities with free-form prompts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) like CLIP show limited performance on satellite imagery due to the underrepresentation of such data in their training sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly contradicts the preprint's positive statement about VLM zero-shot performance on satellite imagery, reframing it as limited due to data underrepresentation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SenCLIP transfers CLIP's representation to Sentinel-2 imagery by leveraging a large dataset of paired images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SenCLIP improves classification accuracy in zero-shot land-use/land-cover mapping tasks by aligning ground-level representations with satellite imagery.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution to emphasize alignment that improves accuracy; this clarifies the mechanism relative to the preprint's description of representation transfer.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SenCLIP demonstrates significant improvements in classification accuracy across both aerial and ground-level prompting styles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SenCLIP improves classification accuracy in zero-shot land-use/land-cover mapping tasks by aligning ground-level representations with satellite imagery.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication assert SenCLIP yields improved classification accuracy; wording differs slightly but the core result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing prompting techniques for satellite imagery are often restricted to generic phrases, limiting their effectiveness for zero-shot land-use and land-cover mapping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing prompting techniques for satellite imagery are often restricted to generic phrases, which limits their effectiveness for zero-shot land-use/land-cover mapping.
alignments.rationale: The limitation of generic prompts for satellite imagery is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sinergym is an open-source Python-based virtual testbed for large-scale building simulation, data collection, continuous control, and experiment monitoring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sinergym is an open-source Python-based virtual testbed for large-scale building simulation, data collection, continuous control, and experiment monitoring. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim with no substantive change in content or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sinergym provides a consistent interface for training and running controllers, predefined benchmarks, experiment visualization and replication support, and comprehensive documentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sinergym provides a consistent interface for training and running controllers, predefined benchmarks, experiment visualization and replication support, and comprehensive documentation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same feature set and support claims verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sinergym demonstrates its applicability for RL-based Building Energy Optimization through several representative examples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sinergym demonstrates its applicability for RL-based Building Energy Optimization through several representative examples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over identically into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of open and standardized tools has hindered the widespread application of Machine Learning and Reinforcement Learning to Building Energy Optimization. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lack of open and standardized tools has hindered the widespread application of Machine Learning and Reinforcement Learning to Building Energy Optimization. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of Gradient Boosted Decision Tree (GBDT) models and Deep Neural Networks (DNN) in an insurance context is evaluated. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models, specifically Gradient Boosted Decision Trees and Deep Neural Networks, are vulnerable to backdoor attacks that can compromise their integrity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed this as an evaluation of robustness; the publication presents a stronger, outcome-focused claim that these models are vulnerable, shifting from neutral evaluation to a definitive vulnerability claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Backdoor attacks can be highly successful on machine learning models, even with a few added samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Backdoor attacks can be highly successful even with a few added samples in the training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same empirical result about attack success with few poisoned samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of backdoor attacks varies between models trained on different datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of backdoor attacks varies depending on the dataset used for training the models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements communicate the same dataset-dependent variability in attack effectiveness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-world attackers face obstacles, but the risk of successful attacks with minimal sample addition should be evaluated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Understanding and mitigating the risks of backdoor attacks is essential for the reliable deployment of machine learning in critical applications like insurance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's cautionary recommendation into a stronger, broader conclusion emphasizing the essential need for understanding and mitigation in critical deployments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study combines empirical data with a feed-forward neural network to predict load capacity and friction coefficients of bolted joints. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model effectively captures nonlinear relationships in bolted joint parameters, achieving 95% predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the model's effectiveness and quantitative performance, clarifying the preprint's methodological contribution into an explicit performance result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves 95.24% predictive accuracy in capturing nonlinear relationships of bolted joints. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model effectively captures nonlinear relationships in bolted joint parameters, achieving 95% predictive accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The accuracy claim is retained in the publication (rounded from 95.24% to 95%) with the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings demonstrate the potential of neural networks as a reliable, efficient alternative for bolted joint design. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings demonstrate the potential of neural networks as a reliable, efficient alternative for bolted joint design. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion about neural networks' potential as an alternative design approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Limited dataset size and diversity restrict the generalizability of the findings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Limited dataset size restricts the generalizability of the model's findings. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding dataset size and resulting generalizability remains present and equivalent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Lower Dimension Bipartite Framework (LDBGF) efficiently embeds network vertices in an interpretable vector space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Lower Dimension Bipartite Framework (LDBGF) efficiently embeds network vertices in an interpretable vector space. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SINr-MF performs well on classical graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SINr-MF can perform well on classical graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same performance claim for SINr-MF on classical graphs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SINr-NR produces high-quality graph and word embeddings that are interpretable and stable across runs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SINr-NR can produce high-quality graph and word embeddings that are interpretable and stable across runs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about SINr-NR's embedding quality and stability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most existing graph embedding methods require long and resource-greedy training and are black-box models. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most graph embedding methods require long and resource greedy training and are black-box, making it complex to understand how the information is structured. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands slightly on the limitation but retains the same core critique about resource demands and opacity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform proposed in this paper significantly reduces the time and effort required to access vital chemical information when datasets follow FAIR principles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform proposed in this paper significantly reduces the time and effort required to access vital chemical information when datasets follow FAIR principles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result claim about time/effort reduction under FAIR datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work aims to improve the accessibility and usability of chemical information by healthcare professionals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work aims to improve the accessibility and usability of chemical information by healthcare professionals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The stated aim regarding healthcare professionals is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The link between hazardous substances and various diseases is demonstrated in scientific studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The link between hazardous substances and various diseases is demonstrated in scientific studies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the result about literature linking hazardous substances to diseases.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform aggregates information from multiple sources and organizes it into a structured knowledge graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform aggregates information from multiple sources and organizes it into a structured knowledge graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the platform's aggregation and knowledge graph is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides recommendations for data owners and publishers to enhance data reuse and interoperability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a recommendations-oriented conclusion not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Rashomon effect presents a significant challenge in model selection due to predictive multiplicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Rashomon effect presents a significant challenge in model selection due to predictive multiplicity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with the same certainty and role, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data-centric AI approaches can mitigate issues related to predictive multiplicity by prioritizing data optimization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-centric AI approaches can mitigate issues related to predictive multiplicity by prioritizing data optimization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint claim exactly in content and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preprocessing methods may inadvertently inflate predictive multiplicity. (certainty=low, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Logistic Regression is presented as the most efficient model for stock market prediction, with FinBERT and GPT-4 representing emerging tools for future exploration. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a correlation between the sentiment indicator and stock price movement. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of GRUvader, an optimal gated recurrent unit network, is proposed for stock market prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Stand-alone models struggled compared with AI-enhanced models in stock market prediction. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New AI twins with fresh cellular level of AI techniques for neuroscience could approximate the brain and its functioning systems with any expected small error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New AI twins with fresh cellular level of AI techniques for neuroscience could approximate the brain and its functioning systems with any expected small error. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI without restrictions could surpass human intelligence with probability one in the end. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI without restrictions could surpass human intelligence with probability one in the end. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper indirectly proves the validity of the conjecture made by Frank Rosenblatt about the potential capabilities of AI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper indirectly proves the validity of the conjecture made by Frank Rosenblatt about the potential capabilities of AI. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper opens new doors for AI techniques to be used in cellular level of efficient neuroscience dynamic analysis and brain illness solutions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of low energy AI techniques can be aided by fundamental neuroscience properties. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning models achieved AUCs of 0.87 for standard MRIs and 0.90 for MRAs, indicating high diagnostic accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved AUCs of 0.87 on standard MRIs and 0.90 on MRAs, indicating high diagnostic accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Bankart lesions were identified in 31.9% of MRAs and 8.6% of standard MRIs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's performance on non-invasive standard MRIs matched or surpassed the radiologists interpreting MRAs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates the feasibility of using deep learning to address the diagnostic challenges posed by subtle pathologies like Bankart lesions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study demonstrates the feasibility of using deep learning to address the diagnostic challenges posed by subtle pathologies like Bankart lesions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models demonstrate potential to improve diagnostic confidence, reduce reliance on invasive imaging, and enhance accessibility to care. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning architecture based on LSTM network surpasses current state-of-the-art models in freezing of gait episode detection. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves an accuracy of 97.71%, sensitivity of 99%, precision of 98%, and specificity of 96% in detecting Parkinson's disease. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LSTM network effectively captures prolonged temporal dependencies in gait patterns, improving the diagnosis of Parkinson's disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LSTM network effectively captures prolonged temporal dependencies in gait patterns, improving the diagnosis of Parkinson's disease. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method eliminates manual feature engineering, which is a limitation of conventional machine learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method eliminates manual feature engineering, which is a limitation of conventional machine learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The statement about removing manual feature engineering is preserved without alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiCat surpasses other methods when benchmarked on 10 publicly available genomic datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCat consistently outperformed existing methods in benchmark evaluations, critically excelling in identifying and distinguishing multiple novel cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiCat demonstrates a capacity to accurately classify novel cell types. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCat demonstrated superior performance in both known cell type classification and novel cell type identification. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid approach of HiCat enhances the embedding learning process and improves the transferability of the supervised model. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCat presents a robust framework for scRNA-seq cell annotation, improving classification accuracy and novel type identification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates the methodological claim to emphasize a robust framework with improved accuracy and identification, amplifying confidence and broadening the claimed impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HiCat effectively merges reference and query genomic data for feature engineering. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HiCat provides a scalable and transferable solution for biomedical research, directly addressing key challenges in automated cell annotation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the technical merging of reference/query data as part of a larger scalable, transferable solution, broadening the contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fuzzy approach to perform norm-based product quantization outperforms existing PQ approaches such as NEQ, PQ, and RQ by achieving a recall of 94%, 69%, and 59% in Netflix, Audio, and Cifar60k datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recall achieved by the proposed method is 94, 69, and 59 for the Netflix, Audio, and Cifar60k datasets, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same dataset-specific recall numbers, preserving the core quantitative result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method improves the recall measure while maintaining low complexity, making it suitable for problems that require querying a large number of potentially relevant items. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fuzzy approach to perform norm-based product quantization (PQ) improves recall measures without compromising complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same trade-off claim (improved recall without added complexity), preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computing time and complexity of the proposed method nearly equals the most computationally efficient existing PQ method in the state-of-the-art. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computing time and complexity of the proposed method are nearly equal to those of the most computationally efficient existing PQ method. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same computational-efficiency comparison without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms seven existing PQ approaches in terms of recall on three public recommender benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific count ('seven') of outperformed approaches and frames broader comparative superiority not present in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A grammar's derivational entropy and the mean length of the utterances (MLU) it generates are fundamentally linked. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A grammar’s derivational entropy and the mean length of the utterances (MLU) it generates are fundamentally linked. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication statement is essentially identical in content and certainty to the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mean length of utterances becomes the most practical index of syntactic complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean length of utterances becomes the most practical index of syntactic complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MLU is not a mere proxy, but a fundamental measure of syntactic diversity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MLU is not a mere proxy, but a fundamental measure of syntactic diversity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim and level of certainty about MLU being a fundamental measure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The derivational entropy rate indexes the rate at which different grammatical annotation frameworks determine the grammatical complexity of treebanks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derivational entropy rate provides a theory-free assessment of grammatical complexity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's framework-comparison indexing into a more general, theory-free assessment and expresses higher certainty, broadening and strengthening the original wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Smoothed Induced Treebank Entropy (SITE) is introduced as a tool for estimating measures accurately, even from very small treebanks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Smoothed Induced Treebank Entropy (SITE) can accurately estimate measures of grammatical complexity even from very small treebanks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about SITE's ability on small treebanks but reports a slightly lower certainty, so content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-Organized Operational Neural Networks (Self-ONNs) can diagnose misalignment and broken rotor faults from thermal images of squirrel-cage induction motors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-Organized Operational Neural Networks (SelfONNs) can diagnose misalignment and broken rotor faults from thermal images of squirrel-cage induction motors. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of Self-ONNs is comparable to that of widely used Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) despite having a shallower architecture. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of Self-ONNs is comparable to that of widely used Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) such as ResNet and EfficientNet. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The streamlined architecture of Self-ONNs ensures high performance and suitability for deployment on edge devices. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The streamlined architecture of Self-ONNs makes them suitable for deployment on edge devices in complex monitoring systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusing infrared (IR) channels with red, green and blue (RGB) channels can enhance feature representation and thus improve crack detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fusing infrared (IR) channels with red, green and blue (RGB) channels can enhance feature representation and improve crack detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed MSCrackMamba paradigm leverages Vision Mamba along with a super-resolution network to address challenges in crack detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed MSCrackMamba paradigm addresses the challenges of aligning IR and RGB channels by applying super-resolution to IR channels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach is validated on the large-scale Crack Detection dataset Crack900, demonstrating an improvement of 3.55% in mIoU compared to the best-performing baseline methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MSCrackMamba approach demonstrates an improvement of 3.55% in mIoU compared to the best-performing baseline methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical validation result is carried over exactly from the preprint to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transformer architecture has proven effective in leveraging increasingly larger amounts of training data when accompanied by an increase in the number of model parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that, under this setting, we can train our models effectively on increasingly larger datasets under a common experimental setup. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We reveal scaling behaviors similar to those found in language models when using the full Amazon Product Data dataset for sequential recommendation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We reveal scaling behaviors similar to those found in language models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compute-optimal training is possible but requires a careful analysis of the compute-performance trade-offs specific to the application. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compute-optimal training is possible but requires a careful analysis of the compute-performance trade-offs specific to the application. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance scaling translates to downstream tasks by fine-tuning larger pre-trained models on smaller task-specific domains. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach and findings provide a strategic roadmap for model training and deployment in real high-dimensional preference spaces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific fine-tuning result into part of a broader strategic roadmap, generalizing the downstream-translatability claim and lowering stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings provide a strategic roadmap for model training and deployment in real high-dimensional preference spaces. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach and findings provide a strategic roadmap for model training and deployment in real high-dimensional preference spaces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This publication claim matches the preprint claim nearly verbatim about providing a strategic roadmap and retains the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced mobile-optimized video diffusion model, MobileVD, is 523x more efficient than previous models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MobileVD generates latents for a 14x512x256 px clip in 1.7 seconds on a Xiaomi-14 Pro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MobileVD can generate latents for a $14 \times 512 \times 256$ px clip in 1.7 seconds on a Xiaomi-14 Pro. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the exact performance measurement and certainty from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of MobileVD shows a slight drop, with FVD scores of 149 compared to 171. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves this performance with negligible quality loss. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reduces memory and computational costs by reducing frame resolution and incorporating multi-scale temporal representations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational cost is reduced by reducing the frame resolution and incorporating multi-scale temporal representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same mechanism and effect for cost reduction with equivalent certainty and scope.
